key_criteria_names: Hands-on Leadership with strategic and cultural transformation capability
Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly

candidates.headline: Leads €300M metals manufacturer with 300 employees across Germany and China production sites.
candidates.motivation_hook: Escaping centralized family ownership to lead global transformation in matrix environment.

scope_match.candidate_actual: €300M at Hirsch, €180M at SMS Group
300 at Hirsch, 120+ at SMS Group
Germany, Italy, India, US, China, Japan at SMS Group; Germany and China at Hirsch
scope_match.role_requirement: Germany, Czech Republic, China, India, US

scope_match.candidate_actual: Automotive, aerospace, defense, general industry machinery at SMS Group; aluminum manufacturing at Hirsch
scope_match.role_requirement: Automotive tier-one suppliers and industrial segments

scope_match.candidate_actual: Grew Italian acquisition from €30M to €80M in 2 years

candidates.scope_seasoning: Tobias brings scope above the role across all dimensions. At SMS Group, he led a €180M business unit with 120+ employees across six countries in a matrix organization. Currently at Hirsch, he manages €300M revenue with 300 employees across German and Chinese production sites. His experience deliberately transforming a German-centric operation into a globally integrated model—reducing German headcount while expanding India from 5 to 30 employees and localizing production to Italy—directly mirrors the transformation StrikoWestofen requires.

key_criteria.name: Hands-on Leadership with strategic and cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: Tobias transformed SMS Group's German-centric operation into a globally integrated model by reducing German workforce, expanding India operations from 5 to 30 employees, and localizing production to Italy. He integrated an acquired Italian company, growing it from €30M to €80M in two years.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Tobias managed 120+ employees across Germany, Italy, India, US, China, and Japan in a matrix organization at SMS Group, with markets evenly split across Europe, North America, and Asia. He deliberately localized products to India for cost competitiveness and is actively leaving Hirsch specifically to escape centralized German family ownership.

key_criteria.name: Similar business size experience
key_criteria.evidence: Tobias led a €180M business unit with 120+ employees at SMS Group and currently manages €300M with 300 employees at Hirsch. He operates hands-on across R&D, sales, and finance without extensive support infrastructure, personally driving technical sales and customer negotiations.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: Tobias led a business unit producing machinery for automotive, aerospace, defense, and general industry at SMS Group, managing the complete value chain from engineering through production to technical sales and installation. Currently serves the aluminum industry (90% of business), providing customer perspective for aluminum foundry equipment.

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Tobias is a native German speaker based near Düsseldorf. He confirmed the 1-2 hour commute to the role location and stated his proven approach of securing a small apartment near the office for regular on-site presence.

compensation.expected_total: Open to discussion — no fixed expectations stated
compensation.flexibility: Demonstrated strong flexibility — open to base + 30% STI + 30% LTI structure. Recruiter assessed current comp as 'right in the sweet spot' for the role.
compensation.notice_period: 6 months to end of month. Realistic earliest start: 3-4 months if negotiation successful.

why_interested.headline: Frustrated by centralized family ownership blocking delegation
why_interested.detail: Actively seeking to leave Hirsch because the owner refuses to delegate authority, creating a traditional German family-owned structure that limits strategic decisions and global expansion.

why_interested.headline: Seeks global matrix environment with transformation mandate
why_interested.detail: Explicitly stated he loves global matrixed organizations and is drawn to transforming German-centric operations into globally integrated businesses — directly aligned with the StrikoWestofen mandate.

potential_concerns.concern: Brief 1.5-year tenure at Trumpf during restructuring — context suggests role misalignment rather than performance issues.
Current role at Hirsch is metals manufacturing rather than capital equipment, representing a sector shift from SMS Group industrial machinery background.

our_take.text: We believe Tobias represents an outstanding cultural and experiential fit for this transformation mandate. His frustration with traditional German family-owned centralization and his demonstrated success building globally integrated operations at SMS Group position him as someone who deeply understands both the current state StrikoWestofen needs to move from and the future state it must achieve. His hands-on leadership across sales, production, and R&D, combined with his comfort in matrix environments, suggests he can drive the required change without requiring extensive support infrastructure.

candidates.flash_summary: Managing Director at Hirsch (€300M, 300 employees). Previously VP Nonferrous Manufacturing at SMS Group — led 120+ across 6 countries, grew Italian acquisition from €30M to €80M in 2 years. Native German speaker near Düsseldorf.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Current total €282K; budget €175K base + 30% STI + 30% LTI ≈ €280K total package. Recruiter confirmed 'sweet spot.'

scope_match.candidate_actual: €12-15M revenue
75 employees
Germany-based, international technology transfer experience (Michigan, Czech Republic, China)
€12-15M at Nico; €4.5M largest single order secured
Food technology machinery; previously automotive machinery at KraussMaffei

key_criteria.name: Strong leadership experience with cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: Turned around underperforming sales operations at Nico and secured the company's largest-ever order (€4.5M with Lorenz Bahlsen), but this was within a 75-person privately-owned organisation over just 2 years — significantly smaller scale than the 170-person PE-backed transformation required.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Led technology transfer initiatives to subsidiaries in Michigan, Czech Republic, Beijing, and Shanghai during 16 years at KraussMaffei, working directly with local engineering teams to build regional technical capability.

key_criteria.name: Comfortable running similar-sized business
key_criteria.evidence: Currently leads a 75-person, €12-15M revenue business at Nico — approximately 20-25% of the StrikoWestofen scope. Previous role at KraussMaffei was technical leadership (Head of Order Engineering, 36 reports) rather than full P&L.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: 16 years in automotive machinery at KraussMaffei (injection moulding, extrusion), followed by 2 years in food technology equipment at Nico. Strong capital equipment manufacturing background across both roles.

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Native German speaker based in Germany. Expressed willingness to relocate or commute, though noted some hesitation about timing given 2-year tenure at current role.

compensation.flexibility: Baseline expectation rather than fixed demand. Acknowledged the task scope is unclear, suggesting openness to discussion. Consultant noted most candidates at €150-170K base; with 30% STI + 30% LTI, packages would exceed €220K.

why_interested.headline: Global transformation mandate appeals
why_interested.detail: Expressed strong interest in the scale and international complexity of the StrikoWestofen opportunity.

why_interested.headline: Outgrowing current scope at Nico
why_interested.detail: Acknowledged the 75-person, €12-15M business may not offer enough growth runway for his ambitions.

potential_concerns.concern: Current P&L scope (€12-15M) is 20-25% of the target role (€60M), with only 2 years in a GM position
Expressed timing hesitation — 'not sure if it is right time already to change again' — which may signal ambivalence about a high-resistance transformation mandate
6-month non-negotiable notice period delays start to approximately October 2025

our_take.text: We believe Sebastian is a solid technical candidate with the right KraussMaffei pedigree and genuine international appetite, but there are real questions about scale readiness and commitment timing. His technology transfer experience is directly relevant to the global integration mandate, however his current GM scope is significantly below what this role demands. The hesitation about timing needs careful probing in any follow-up.

candidates.flash_summary: General Manager at Nico with 16 years of automotive machinery experience at KraussMaffei. Led technology transfer to international subsidiaries but current P&L scope (€12-15M) is 20-25% of the target role.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Expects €220K; budget range of €175K base + 30% STI + 30% LTI exceeds expectation

candidates.current_title: Managing Director for Sales and Technology

scope_match.candidate_actual: Dual MD with full commercial responsibility; revenue not explicitly stated
130 employees across tool and machine tool divisions
Solingen-based, international sales operations
Not explicitly stated; 130-employee industrial machinery business
Industrial machinery — machine tools and cutting tools for automotive and aviation

key_criteria.name: Strong leadership experience with cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: 15-year tenure at FORST Technologie has been characterised by building and expanding within a stable family-owned environment rather than driving transformation in resistant settings. Progressed from Head of Production to MD but has not demonstrated experience restructuring centralised decision-making cultures.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Led successful aviation market entry and international sales expansion at FORST Technologie, but operations remain Germany-centred with limited evidence of managing distributed international production teams.

key_criteria.name: Comfortable running similar-sized business
key_criteria.evidence: Leads 130-employee dual division at FORST Technologie as Managing Director, with hands-on operational involvement across sales, technology, and production — close to the target scale.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: Deep industrial machinery expertise with 15 years at FORST Technologie spanning machine tools, cutting tools for automotive and aviation sectors. Drove 75% CO2 footprint reduction through retrofit tool design.

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Native German speaker based in Solingen area, within commuting distance of Wiehl. Strong preference for on-site presence demonstrated in current role.

compensation.flexibility: Current total approximately €235,000 including car and pension. Expectations to be confirmed after learning more about opportunity scope and structure.
compensation.notice_period: 3 months contractual, tied to June 30 business year

why_interested.headline: Scale and global scope attractive
why_interested.detail: Interested in the step up from single-site family ownership to a PE-backed global technology platform.

why_interested.headline: Limited growth ceiling at FORST
why_interested.detail: 15 years at the same company suggests readiness for a new challenge beyond the family-owned environment.

potential_concerns.concern: Pure Mittelstand background — exactly the profile the client asked to avoid — with centralised leadership style and no experience in PE-backed matrix environments
No demonstrated cultural transformation experience in resistant organisations; career has been building within a stable environment rather than changing one

our_take.text: We see a capable technical leader with strong operational instincts and a proven innovation track record, but significant concerns exist about cultural fit for the transformation mandate. His pure Mittelstand background — exactly what the client flagged as a risk — and preference for centralised, hands-on control contrast with the delegation and global integration the role demands. Proceed with caution.

candidates.flash_summary: Managing Director at FORST Technologie for 15 years, leading 130 employees across industrial machinery for automotive and aviation. Strong product innovation (75% CO2 reduction) but pure Mittelstand background with no matrix or PE experience.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Current total ~€235K; budget structure would match or exceed

candidates.current_company: Currently between roles (exited Spear in December 2024)

scope_match.candidate_actual: €75M at Spear; grew Vollert from €60M to €90M
400 at Spear; 300 at Vollert
Built production in India, Poland, Thailand; managed sales in China and USA
Grew Vollert from €60M to €90M over tenure
Specialty commercial vehicles (Spear), industrial machinery / heavy logistics equipment (Vollert), coating machinery (Coatema)

key_criteria.name: Strong leadership experience with cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: At Spear, delivered the company's best two years by professionalising purchasing, engineering, and cost reduction. At Vollert, introduced Lean management, Eastern European sourcing, and standardisation while building international production. Explicitly stated leadership philosophy of empowerment over micromanagement.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Built production facilities in India, Poland, and Thailand at Vollert, managed sales operations in China and USA, and drove internationalisation of a previously Germany-focused business.

key_criteria.name: Comfortable running similar-sized business
key_criteria.evidence: Led €75M / 400-employee business at Spear and grew Vollert from €60M to €90M with 300 employees — directly comparable to the €60M / 170-FTE StrikoWestofen scope.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: Career spanning industrial machinery (Vollert — heavy logistics equipment), specialty vehicles (Spear), and coating machinery (Coatema). Deep R&D and engineering credentials with 65 patents to his name.

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Native German speaker based in Steinheim, NRW — within commuting distance of Wiehl. Currently between roles and available immediately.

compensation.flexibility: Did not indicate compensation would be a barrier. Currently between roles, which may increase flexibility.

why_interested.headline: Transformation mandate matches sweet spot
why_interested.detail: The scale, international complexity, and PE-backed growth agenda directly align with his Vollert experience.

why_interested.headline: Available immediately after Spear exit
why_interested.detail: Currently between roles following December 2024 departure, actively seeking the right next leadership opportunity.

potential_concerns.concern: Pattern of 2-5 year tenures with two departures linked to post-acquisition or organisational changes — may indicate difficulty with ownership transitions
Most recent role at Spear was more regionally focused (DACH) than globally operational, though Vollert experience was strongly international

our_take.text: We see Deimel as a highly compelling candidate with exceptional energy and executive presence. His Vollert experience mirrors the target role almost exactly — growing an industrial machinery business from €60M to €90M while building international production operations. His technical credentials (65 patents), immediate availability, and hands-on global operations experience position him as a potential strong fit for the transformation mandate.

candidates.flash_summary: Experienced industrial MD currently available, having grown Vollert from €60M to €90M revenue while building production in India, Poland, and Thailand. 65 patents. Immediate start. Scope directly mirrors the target role.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Current €250K at Spear; budget structure competitive and candidate between roles

scope_match.candidate_actual: €8-10M at Steinert; €60-65M at Bühler Group; grew Kaiser Filter Tech €30M to €50M
30 at Steinert; 30-35 at Bühler; 300 at Kaiser Filter Tech
Multi-country experience across Europe, Africa, Asia; led operations in South Africa, Middle East, Turkey
€60-65M business unit at Bühler Group
Industrial machinery — screening/sorting (Steinert), food processing (Bühler), filtration (Kaiser Filter Tech)

key_criteria.name: Strong leadership experience with cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: At Kaiser Filter Tech, led comprehensive cultural change and turnaround of unprofitable operations, growing from €30M to €50M. Explicitly challenged German-centric headquarters mentality — advocates decentralisation based on local needs.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Exceptional multicultural experience across Europe, Africa, and Asia at Bühler Group, including South Africa, Middle East, and Turkey. Fluent in multiple languages with proven ability to empower regional teams.

key_criteria.name: Comfortable running similar-sized business
key_criteria.evidence: Led €60-65M business unit at Bühler Group with 30-35 employees and grew Kaiser Filter Tech from €30M to €50M with 300 employees across multiple manufacturing sites.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: 25 years of continuous experience in industrial machinery and capital equipment — screening and sorting (Steinert/Mogensen), food processing equipment (Bühler), and industrial filtration (Kaiser Filter Tech).

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Fluent German speaker based in Hamburg. Willing to relocate or commute for the right opportunity, with established pattern of on-site operational leadership.

compensation.flexibility: Pragmatic approach — stated compensation is 'part of the job' rather than primary driver. Acknowledged client's proposed base + 30% STI + 30% LTI structure without objection.

why_interested.headline: Transformation mandate is his sweet spot
why_interested.detail: The challenge of shifting from German-centric to globally integrated operations directly matches his career thesis.

why_interested.headline: Constrained by ownership turmoil at Steinert
why_interested.detail: Five ownership changes in under three years including insolvency have limited his ability to execute a long-term strategy.

potential_concerns.concern: Strong reservations about automotive industry exposure — stated it 'gives me a lot of bad days' — despite role's 50%+ automotive revenue base
Current scope at Steinert (€8-10M, 30 people) is well below peak; real comparable experience is 4+ years dated at Bühler/Kaiser

our_take.text: We believe Armatys is a standout candidate who interviews exceptionally well and brings the right combination of hands-on leadership, multicultural sophistication, and resilience to drive the transformation Norican needs. His commanding executive presence, proven ability to navigate complex organisational transitions, and explicit philosophy of decentralisation directly address the brief. The automotive reservation warrants discussion but does not disqualify.

candidates.flash_summary: Managing Director at Steinert with 25 years in industrial machinery. Led €60-65M business unit at Bühler Group and grew Kaiser Filter Tech from €30M to €50M. Exceptional multicultural leader who explicitly challenges German-centric mindsets.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Current €240-250K; budget competitive and compensation not a primary driver

scope_match.candidate_actual: €20-25M at MSB; previously €170M at Bielomatik
~100 at MSB; 800-900 at Bielomatik
Led operations across Germany, Italy, China, and international locations at Bielomatik
€170M at Bielomatik; €20-25M current at MSB
Industrial machinery — conveying systems for paper, automotive, pharmaceutical (MSB); packaging/welding (Bielomatik)

key_criteria.name: Strong leadership experience with cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: At Bielomatik, converted a German export business into a globally integrated operation, establishing international subsidiaries and growing China production from 30 to 200+ employees. At MSB, overcame significant resistance from family shareholders.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Managed multi-site international operations across Germany, Italy, China at Bielomatik, driving technology transfer from Germany to China and establishing global production model — directly mirrors the StrikoWestofen mandate.

key_criteria.name: Comfortable running similar-sized business
key_criteria.evidence: Led €170M / 800-900 employee business at Bielomatik, significantly exceeding the target scope. Current €20-25M role at MSB demonstrates comfort operating at smaller scale too.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: Career spanning industrial machinery and capital equipment — conveying systems at MSB, packaging/welding technology at Bielomatik. Deep sector credibility in German industrial manufacturing.

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Native German speaker based in Düren — within reasonable commuting distance of Wiehl. Eight years at current single-site role demonstrates commitment to on-site leadership.

compensation.flexibility: Indicated current base of €190K and bonus of €50K; likely seeking uplift to justify leaving an 8-year CEO position.
compensation.notice_period: 6-9 months (best case 6 months to end of quarter)

why_interested.headline: Global transformation role is compelling
why_interested.detail: The mandate to shift from German-centric to globally integrated operations directly mirrors his Bielomatik experience.

why_interested.headline: Eight years at single-location MSB
why_interested.detail: May have reached the ceiling of what the family-owned, single-site business can offer.

potential_concerns.concern: Large-scale international matrix leadership experience at Bielomatik is eight years dated; current role lacks the multi-site, PE-backed complexity required
6-9 month notice period could delay start significantly

our_take.text: We believe Voessing's direct experience transforming German-centric operations into global businesses at Bielomatik, combined with his proven ability to overcome cultural resistance and manage multi-site international teams, makes him a strong strategic fit for the StrikoWestofen mandate. The key question is whether his current eight-year tenure at a single-site €20-25M business has dulled the operational intensity needed for this transformation.

candidates.flash_summary: CEO of MSB Maschinenbau for eight years. Previously led €170M / 800-person Bielomatik operation, converting a German export business into a globally integrated operation — directly mirrors the StrikoWestofen mandate.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Current ~€240K; budget structure would be competitive

scope_match.candidate_actual: €145M at Frimo Group; €230M at Motherson
900 employees at Frimo across 6 countries; 1,200 at Motherson
Multi-country: Germany, Hungary, China, USA, Mexico at Frimo; global at Motherson
€145M P&L at Frimo Group
Capital equipment for automotive, aerospace, furniture, and industrial markets

key_criteria.name: Strong leadership experience with cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: At Frimo Group, shifted production from two of four German sites to Hungary and China, repositioning Germany as a technology and innovation centre. At Motherson, led turnaround of a €230M / 1,200-employee operation acquired out of insolvency with significant workforce restructuring.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Manages operations across six countries at Frimo Group including Germany, Hungary, China, USA, and Mexico. Previously drove global integration at Motherson including technology transfer and international production establishment.

key_criteria.name: Comfortable running similar-sized business
key_criteria.evidence: Currently CTO with full P&L responsibility for €145M revenue and 900 employees at Frimo Group — significantly exceeding the target scope. Comfortable operating at both larger (Motherson €230M) and smaller scales.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: 40 years in capital equipment manufacturing spanning Frimo Group (tooling for automotive/aerospace), Motherson (automotive components), and prior roles. Deep engineering and operational credentials.

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Native German speaker based in Osnabrück. Open to relocation and comfortable with the Monday-Friday commuting arrangement the role requires.

compensation.flexibility: Consultant noted client can 'absolutely make a competitive offer.' Current package of €210K is below the role's budget range, suggesting compensation would not be a barrier.

why_interested.headline: Technology centre model is his expertise
why_interested.detail: The mandate to position StrikoWestofen as a technology and innovation centre directly mirrors what he is executing at Frimo.

why_interested.headline: Seeking right-sized P&L ownership
why_interested.detail: Current CTO title may understate his operational scope; the SVP role offers clear P&L ownership at an appropriate scale.

potential_concerns.concern: Entire 40-year career within German-headquartered industrial organisations may limit ability to drive the cultural shift from 'German exporter' to 'global technology centre' mindset
CTO title rather than MD/CEO may indicate technology-first rather than commercial-first leadership orientation

our_take.text: We believe Janotta brings proven operational transformation credentials at directly relevant scale — his work shifting Frimo's production from Germany to Hungary and China while positioning Germany as a technology centre is essentially the StrikoWestofen playbook. His 40-year German industrial career is both a strength (deep credibility) and a flag (potential mindset limitation). Compensation would not be a barrier given the step-up from his current €210K package.

candidates.flash_summary: CTO at Frimo Group with €145M P&L and 900 employees across six countries. Currently shifting production from Germany to Hungary and China while positioning Germany as a technology centre — essentially the StrikoWestofen playbook.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Current €210K is below budget range; significant step-up available

candidates.flash_summary: Division Manager at Yaskawa with full P&L responsibility for €100-150M revenue and 280 direct reports. Proven operational leader across multi-site international industrial businesses. Originally from Düsseldorf area — location is a strong pull factor.
candidates.compensation_alignment_note: Current €185K base + 40% bonus; budget structure is competitive and compensation not the primary driver

scope_match.candidate_actual: €100-150M revenue at Yaskawa (varies by project cycle)
280 direct reports within 400-person facility; plus extended workbench in Slovenia
Munich-based; managed 5 locations across Germany, Netherlands, Sweden, Czech Republic at Parker Hannifin
€100-150M at Yaskawa; €80M at Parker Hannifin
Industrial robotics and automation systems for automotive, general industry, healthcare, and construction

key_criteria.name: Strong leadership experience with cultural transformation capability
key_criteria.evidence: Led operational restructuring at Parker Hannifin including closing the Netherlands plant and consolidating to France, managing a 3-shift operation. At Yaskawa, manages 280 people across all functions. However, limited evidence of driving cultural transformation in resistant, entrenched organisations — experience is more operational turnaround than mindset change.

key_criteria.name: Global mindset and international experience
key_criteria.evidence: Managed international teams across five locations in Germany, Netherlands, Sweden, and Czech Republic at Parker Hannifin. At Yaskawa, oversees extended operations including an external workbench in Slovenia.

key_criteria.name: Comfortable running similar-sized business
key_criteria.evidence: Full P&L ownership of €100-150M revenue with 280 direct reports at Yaskawa, and previously €80M at Parker Hannifin — significantly exceeding the €60M / 170-FTE target scope.

key_criteria.name: Industrial background
key_criteria.evidence: Career spanning industrial robotics and automation at Yaskawa, actuators and motion systems at Parker Hannifin. Deep capital equipment and custom systems engineering background across automotive and general industry sectors.

key_criteria.name: Fluent German speaker with ability to be on-site in Wiehl regularly
key_criteria.evidence: Native German speaker, originally from the Düsseldorf area. Confirmed location is not an issue — estimated 30-minute commute from east Düsseldorf to site. Relocation from Munich to home region is a positive pull factor.

compensation.expected_total: €175-185K base range; seeking clarity on 30% STI + 30% LTI structure
compensation.flexibility: Current comp is above the stated budget base (€185K vs €175K), but she did not indicate compensation is the primary driver. Location and international mandate appear more important. No explicit deal-breakers stated.

why_interested.headline: Returning to home region near Düsseldorf
why_interested.detail: Originally from the Düsseldorf area and confirmed the Wiehl location is attractive — sees it as a homecoming rather than a relocation.

why_interested.headline: International transformation mandate appeals
why_interested.detail: Interested in the global scope and technology centre positioning of the StrikoWestofen role.

potential_concerns.concern: Motivation appears more location-driven than transformation-driven — the cultural change mandate may be underestimated given her collaborative style
Limited evidence of navigating entrenched resistance and shifting centralised mindsets; experience is operational turnaround and restructuring rather than cultural transformation

our_take.text: We believe Rebecca is a strong operational leader with the right scale and international experience, but her motivation appears more location-driven than transformation-driven. The cultural change mandate may be underestimated given her collaborative style and lack of evidence navigating entrenched resistance. Her P&L scale significantly exceeds the target, and the Düsseldorf proximity is a genuine retention advantage.
